mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to increased wage pressures and subsequently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over 2 months indicates an increase in supply chain activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in the Housing Starts (HOUST) over 3 months indicates a decrease in demand for housing, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in decreasing demand periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) over 2 months indicates a tightening monetary cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) over 2 months indicates an increase in labor market activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in increasing labor market activity periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Disposable Personal Income (RSAFS) over 2 months indicates an increase in consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.
